summary: Patched the marketing loop to self-heal stale latest-truth aliases and immediately refreshed the live distribution-lane and outcome-execution-board snapshots so future runs no longer inherit the old 2026-05-25 guard-pause state.
why_now: distribution_lane_latest.json and outcome_execution_board_latest.json were still pinned to the 2026-05-25 distribution_architecture_guard_pause state even though the live 2026-05-28 board/hold artifacts had moved on.
That alias drift could mislead repair logic and any future hold-window reruns into treating stale guard-pause truth as current.
The highest-leverage truthful action in this slot was a runtime repair plus an immediate alias resync, not another fake-progress packet refresh.
shared_findings_reused: distribution_lane_latest.json: stale 2026-05-25 guard-pause alias before repair
outcome_execution_board_latest.json: stale 2026-05-25 outcome snapshot before repair
marketing_2026-05-28.json: latest hold execution log showing current lane truth

behavior: Added a stale-latest-truth detector for outcome_execution_board_latest.* alongside the existing distribution_lane_latest.* guard.
Added a self-healing snapshot refresh path that calls outcome_execution_board_runner.sync_latest_truth_snapshot whenever the latest truth aliases drift from the current board/lane decision.
Applied the self-heal in both active-hold and standard run paths before follow-through scheduling/execution so future runs refresh aliases automatically.
